original: English pilgrim Willibald (later St. Willibald) arrives in Jerusalem. One of the first known Europeans to visit the Holy Land.
The end of the Carolingian Empire: King Charles III resigns as Emperor after a revolt led by his nephew Arnulf, the empire is then split up
Otto IV, nephew of Richard the Lionheart of England, is elected Holy Roman Emperor at Frankfurt
4th Lateran Council (12th ecumenical council) opens in Rome
Oddo Colonna elected as Pope Martinus V
Treaty of Granada: France & Aragon divide the Kingdom of Naples
Duke of Alva's son Don Fredrik begins siege of Haarlem
Turkey and Austria sign the Treaty of Zsitva-Torok
Mayflower Pilgrims make their first landing in America, at Provincetown Harbor, Massachusetts
Following pressure from Anglican bishop John Atherton, the Irish House of Commons passes "An Act for the Punishment for the Vice of Buggery"
Thomas Wentworth, Earl of Strafford, impeached by the House of Lords on the evidence of John Pym, and imprisoned in the Tower of London; he was later executed.
Massachusetts passes first compulsory school attendance law in the American colonies
Dutch & French agree to divide St Maarten, Leeward Islands
Dutch States-General forbids importation of French wine

Second Battle of Khotyn in the Ukraine, Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth forces under the command of Jan Sobieski. defeat the Ottoman army. In this battle, rockets of Kazimierz Siemienowicz were successfully used.
A highway in Bronx is laid out, later renamed East 233rd Street
First collegiate secret society in the United States, the F.H.C. Society (Flat Hat Club), is formed at Raleigh Tavern in Williamsburg, Virginia
Theresianische Military Academy opens in Vienna
British Soldiers and Loyalists, allied with Iroquois and Seneca raiders, slaughter 40 in the "Cherry Valley Massacre" in central New York
Chrysanthemums are introduced to England from China
Cartagena Colombia declares independence from Spain
Dresden surrenders to allied armies
Chile declares war on Bolivia & Peru
The Virginia Military Institute is founded in Lexington, Virginia.
Alvan Clark patents telescope
Skirmish at Shoal Creek, AL
Mary Edward Walker, the first US Army female surgeon, awarded Medal of Honor
Thomas William Robertson's play "Society" premieres in London
Treaty of Sinchula is signed in which Bhutan ceded the areas east of the Teesta River to the British East India Company.
1st American amateur track & field meet (NYC)
Anarchist Haymarket Martyrs August Spies (b. 1855), Albert Parsons (b. 1848), Adolph Fischer (b. 1858) and George Engel (b. 1836) are executed.
Construction of the Manchester Ship Canal starts at Eastham.
Washington admitted as 42nd state of USA
D McCree patents portable fire escape
Bechuanaland becomes part of Cape Colony
Jules Vandenpeereboom becomes Belgium's minister of War
Samuel Pierpont Langley's Number 6 'heavier-than-air' aircraft model flies over 1,500 m (5,000 ft).
Stuart/Rubens/Boyd-Jones' "Floradora" premieres in London
High Commissioner Prince George declares amnesty for all leaders of the insurrection that has been disturbing Crete during the recent months - but which never gained mass support
Ethel Smyth's "Standrecht" premieres in Leipzig
Construction of US navy base begins at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii
J M Synge's "Tinker's Wedding" premieres in London
Many cities in the U.S. Midwest broke their record highs and lows on the same day as a strong cold front rolls through. (see The 11/11/11 cold wave).
Russia issues an ultimatum to Persia and follows it with an invasion of North Persia to impose political control
Dutch SDAP leader Troelstra announces revolution
Emperor Charles I of Austria-Hungary abdicates
Pope Benedictus XV states Roman Catholics political and business views
Great Britain's monument to her war dead, the Cenotaph in Whitehall, designed by Edwin Lutyens, unveiled
The burials of unknown soldiers take place simultaneously in Westminster Abbey in London, and at the Arc de Triomphe in Paris
Largest US flag displayed (150' X 90') expanded in 1939 (270' X 90')
Eternal flame lit for the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier under the Arc de Triomphe in Paris
Martin Beck Theater opens at 302 W 45th St NYC
Palace of Legion of Honor dedicated in San Francisco
City of Chicago, Illinois renames Municipal Grant Park Stadium, as Soldier Field, in honor of US soldiers killed in combat during World War I
Earnest Thalmann becomes chairman of German KPD
Night of Kersten - Prime Minister Hendrikus Colijn's Dutch government falls by SGP-amendement
U.S. Route 66 is established from Chicago, Illinois to Santa Monica, California 2,448 miles (3,940 km)
KXO-AM in El Centro CA begins radio transmissions
WGL-AM in Ft Wayne IN begins radio transmissions
WMT-AM in Cedar Rapids IA begins radio transmissions
WOL-AM in Washington, D.C. begins radio transmissions
Cornerstones laid for Opera House & Veteran's Building in San Francisco, California
"Great Black Blizzard" 1st of the great dust storms that created the dust bowl rips through South Dakota
WOC-AM in Davenport Iowa splits from WHO-WOC & becomes KICK-AM
Explorer 2 balloon sets altitude record of 72,000 feet over South Dakota
German aircraft Messerschmidt ME-109V13 flies a new world air speed record for landplanes with piston engines of 610.95 km/h (379.62 mph)
Nobel prize for physics awarded to American Clinton Joseph Davisson and Briton George Paget Thomson "for their experimental discovery of the diffraction of electrons by crystals"
Kristallnacht: German authorities fine Jewish population of Germany and Austria 1 billion Marks, blaming the community for inciting the violence against them
Blizzard strikes midwestern US killing over 100
British Fleet Air Arm attack destroys half of Italian fleet at Taranto
Thousands of Paris students lay a wreath at the Grave of the Unknown Soldier
Willys unveiled its General Purpose vehicle ("Jeep")
Czech premier General Eliasj arrested by Nazis
-12] last German offensive in Stalingrad
Germany completes its WWII occupation of France
Jews in the Free Zone of France ordered to wear a yellow star of David
Lt-general Kumakashi Harada becomes Japanese commander on Java
Transport #45 departs with French Jews to Nazi-Germany
US air raid on Rabaul, Papua New Guinea
New York Rangers beat Detroit Red Wings, 5-2 to end NHL record 25 game winless streak (0-21-4)
New York Knicks play their first Basketball Association of America (BAA) home game at Madison Square Garden, losing 78-68 to the Chicago Stags in overtime
WTTV TV channel 4 in Bloomington-Indianapol, IN (IND) 1st broadcast
Demolition begins on cable car barn at California & Hyde, San Francisco
AL announces Kansas City will play AL record 52 night games in 1959
Seals Stadium, a minor league baseball park and temporary home of MLB Giants) in San Francisco, California demolished
Record New York Knicks' crowd for the third Madison Square Garden venue on 49th Street, NYC; 18,499 see Knicks beat Syracuse Nationals, 112-108
"Catch-22" by Joseph Heller is published by Simon and Schuster in New York
Adulterous couple ride a dung cart through Staphorst, Netherlands
City of Stalingrad renamed Volgograd
Congolese soldiers murder 13 Italian UN pilots
Kuwait's National Assembly ratifies the Constitution of Kuwait
Rhodesian Prime Minister Ian Smith signs Unilateral Declaration of Independence, proclaiming independence from Britain
William Alfred's "Hogan's Goat" premieres in NYC
Methodist Church & Evangelical United Brethren Church unite as United Methodist Church (USA)
NASA launches spaceship Gemini 12
Maldives (in Indian Ocean) becomes a republic
Ron Hill sets record 10-mile run (46:44) at Leicester England
Baltimore Oriole Boog Powell wins AL MVP
Man-made earthslide at Kawasaki Japan, kills 15
Dow Jones Industrial Average moves above 1,000 for 1st time
US Army turns over Long Bihn base to South Vietnamese army
Angola gains independence from Portugal (National Day)
Australian Prime Minister Gough Whitlam is removed from office by Governor-General Sir John Kerr, the first elected Prime Minister removed in Australian history
James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement (the first "modern treaty") signed between Quebec government and Grand Council of the Crees of Quebec and the Northern Quebec Inuit Association
Wings release "Mull of Kintyre" & "Girl's School"
Maumoon Abdul Gayoom becomes president of Maldives
Boston Court issues occupancy permit for Cambridge Buddhist Center
Crew of Soyuz 35 returns to Earth aboard Soyuz 37
5th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Columbia makes the first officially "operational" shuttle mission
Gas explosion in Israeli army headquarters near Tyre; kills 60
Australian cricket wicketkeeper/batsman Wayne Phillips scores 159 on Test debut vs Pakistan at WACA, Perth
First US cruise missiles arrive in the United Kingdom
Challenger flies back to Kennedy Space Center via Davis-Monthan Air Force Base
First AIDS-themed TV movie "An Early Frost" screens in the US on NBC
Yonkers is found guilty of segregating schools and housing
Houston's Astro Mike Scott (18-10) wins NL Cy Young Award
Suriname government proclaims gold purification
Moscow party secretary Boris Jerusalem resigns
Oldest known insect fossils (390 million years old) are reported in the journal Science
Romanian students protest in Bucharest before the Communist Party congress, shouting "we want reforms", in a sign of the revolution to come
California Angel Chuck Finley & Seattle Mariner Randy Johnson combine to pitch a no-hitter in exhibition game between US & Japanese all-star teams
The Church of England approves the ordination of female priests
Progress M-25 launched to space station Mir
Atlanta Braves starting pitcher John Smoltz wins NL Cy Young Award; Pat Hentgen of the Toronto Blue Jays claims AL Award
Canadian based players win Cy Young Awards for best MLB pitcher; Toronto Blue Jays'Roger Clemenswins his 4th AL Award; Pedro Martinez of Montreal Expos takes NL Award
NHL’s new Columbus franchise (scheduled to begin play in 2000) announce team’s name would be “Blue Jackets” after soldiers in the Union army during the American Civil War
WNBA announces franchises in Detroit (Shock) and Washington D.C. (Mystics) would join the League as expansion teams for the 1998 season
Last upside down date until January 1, 6000
155 skiers and snowboarders die when a funicular railway catches fire in an alpine tunnel near Kaprun, Austria
Foreign journalists Pierre Billaud, Johanne Sutton, and Volker Handloik are killed in Afghanistan during an attack by Taliban troops on the convoy they are traveling with
New Zealand Tomb of the Unknown Warrior dedicated at the National War Memorial, Wellington
New Zealand war memorial monument unveiled by Her MajestyQueen Elizabeth IIin London, United Kingdom, commemorating the loss of soldiers from the New Zealand Army and the British Army
RMS Queen Elizabeth 2 (QE2) sets sail from Southampton, England on her final voyage to Dubai
12 people are killed by a magnitude 6.8 earthquake in Burma
100 people are killed in a tropical cyclone in the Puntland region, Somalia
4 people are killed and 8 are injured after a building catches fire in Mumbai, India
58 people are killed in a bus crash in the Sukkur District, Pakistan
An Italian appeals court overturns a manslaughter conviction against 6 scientists for failing to give adequate warning of a deadly earthquake
Samsung Lions beat Nexen Heroes, 11-1 in Game 6 at Jamsil Baseball Stadium, Seoul to win the Korean Series
The captain of the South Korean ferry which sank in April is found guilty of gross negligence and sentenced to 36 years in prison

Congress of Carnuntum: Attempting to keep peace within the Roman Empire, the leaders of the Tetrarchy declareMaxentiusand Licinius to be Augusti, while rival contenderConstantine Iis declared Caesar of Britain and Gaul
EmperorFrederick IBarbarossa declares himself ruler of North Italy
Battle of Aleppo:Timurand his army defeat the forces of Sultan Faraj, Mameluke ruler of Egypt, 20,000 people reportedly massacred and a pyramid of their skulls built
ExplorerChristopher Columbussights the island of Saba in the Leeward islands, but does not land
Julius IIelected pope - rules till his death in 1513, one of the most powerful popes of the Renaissance
French KingHenry IVbegins his unsuccessful siege of Rouen, capital of Normandy
German mathematicianGottfried Wilhelm Leibnizdemonstrates integral calculus for the first time to find the area under the graph of the function y = f(x)
Prince William of Orange's invasion fleet sails to England
Bonnie Prince Charlie's Jacobite army invades England in an attempt to restore the House of Stuart to the British throne

Mohawk military leaderJoseph Brantgoes to London to solicit more support from the government and to persuade the Crown to address past Mohawk land grievances in exchange for their participation as allies in the impending war
Napoleonic Wars: Battle of Dürenstein - 8,000 French troops attempt to slow the retreat of a vastly superior Russian and Austrian force
Washington Irving's Salmagundi periodical is published, first to associate the name "Gotham" with New York City
US Civil War: UnionGeneral Sherman's troops destroy Rome, Georgia
War of the Triple Alliance: Allied victory in the Battle of Avay leaves 3,000 Paraguayan soldiers dead, 600 wounded and the road to Asunción open
Australian bushranger, gang leader and outlaw legendNed Kellyis hanged at Melbourne Gaol
Maurice Ravel's piano composition "Jeux d'eau" premieres
Independence of Poland proclaimed byJózef Piłsudski
US PresidentWarren G. Hardingdedicates Tomb of Unknown Soldier at Arlington Cemetery
American scientistRobert A. Millikanannounces discovery of cosmic rays
Louis Armstrongand his Hot Five begin their first recording session at Okeh Records in Chicago
Future Baseball Hall of Fame second basemanEddie Collinsis released as Chicago White Sox player/manager; replaced by another future HOF'er catcher Ray Schalk
France's 5th government ofRaymond Poincaréforms
Patent number US1781541 is awarded toAlbert EinsteinandLeó Szilárdfor their invention of the Einstein refrigerator
Billie Holiday's second song and first hit, "Riffin' the Scotch", is released
Kate Smith first singsIrving Berlin's "God Bless America"
745 French Jews deported toAuschwitz
WWII: British led Allied forces defeatErwin Rommel's German and Italian Army Panzers in Second Battle of El-Alamein, North Africa, helping to secure the Suez Canal
New York Yankees pitcher Spud Chandler wins AL MVP; St. Louis Cardinals outfielderStan Musialwins NL MVP
"Gentlemen's Agreement" directed by Elia Karan and starringGregory Peckand Dorothy McGuire premieres in New York City (Best Picture 1948)
Publication of "Two Towers", 2nd volume of "Lord of the Rings", byJ. R. R. Tolkienby George Allen and Unwin in London
Sun Record's release single "Great Balls of Fire" byJerry Lee Lewis; sells a million copies in first ten day
Stalinists Anti-Party GroupMolotov,Malenkovand Kaganovitsj expelled from USSR's communist party for opposing liberalization
NASA Gemini 12 launches on a 4-day flight withJim LovellandBuzz Aldrin
John Lennonand Yoko Ono appear nude on cover of "Two Virgins" album
Apple Records releases single "Get Back" by 'The Beatles withBilly Preston' in UK
Jim Morrisonarrested on an airplane by the FBI for drunkenness
Operatic sopranoMaria Callasmakes her final public appearance in Sapporo, Japan
Liberal leaderMalcolm Frasersworn in as caretaker Prime Minister of Australia after sacking of the Whitlam Government by Governor General John Kerr
NY Islanders' future Hockey Hall of Fame right wingMike Bossyscores 4 goals in 6-6 tie against visiting Minnesota North Stars
MLB Los Angeles Dodgers pitcherFernando Valenzuelabecomes first MLB rookie to win a Cy Young Award; Milwaukee Brewers' Rollie Fingers takes AL Award
Joe Altobelli succeeds retiringEarl Weaveras Baltimore Orioles manager
President Reaganbecame 1st US President to address Japanese legislature
Boston Red Sox starterRoger Clemenswins back-to-back AL Cy Young Awards; Philadelphia Phillies starter Steve Bedrosian takes NL Award
JudgeAnthony Kennedynominated to US Supreme Court by PresidentRonald Reagan
Van Gogh's "Irises" sells for record $53.6 million at auction
Krzysztof Pendereckiconducts premiere of his revised version of "A Polish Requiem" in Stockholm, Sweden
PopeJohn Paul IIis hospitalized for two days for a fractured shoulder
Bill Gatesbuys Leonardo da Vinci's "Codex" for $30,800,000 - then the most expensive manuscript ever sold
Gothic horror film "Interview with a Vampire", based on the book byAnne Rice, directed by Neal Jordan is released, starringTom Cruise,Brad PittandChristian Slater
CBS News anchorDan Ratherrenews his contract to 2002
Belgian tennis starKim Clijstersbeats AmericanSerena Williams7–5, 6–3 to win the season-ending WTA Tour Championship at the Staples Center, Los Angeles
Josh Grobanreleases his second album "Closer"; it goes to #1 on US charts and becomes his biggest seller
Yasser Arafat's death from unidentified causes is confirmed by the Palestine Liberation Organization, andMahmoud Abbasis elected PLO chairman shortly after
Justine Heninof Belgium beats RussianMaria Sharapova5–7, 7–5, 6–3 to win $1m winners cheque and her 2nd straight season-ending WTA Tour Championship in Madrid, Spain
Taylor Swiftreleases her second studio album "Fearless" (Billboard Album of the Year, 2009; Grammy Album of the Year, 2010, American Music Awards, 2009)
"The Hunger Games: Catching Fire" directed by Francis Lawrence starringJennifer LawrenceandJosh Hutchersonpremieres in London
Novak Đokovićclaims back-to-back ATP World Tour Finals tennis titles with a 6-3, 6-4 win over SpaniardRafael Nadalin the final in London, England
Mayflower Compact is signed by Pilgrims at Cape Cod, establishing the first framework of government in the territory that is now the USA [N.S. Nov 21]
World War I Armistice signed by the Allies and Germany comes into effect, with hostilities ending at 11 a.m., "the eleventh hour of the eleventh day of the eleventh month"
Brian Epstein andEd Sullivansign a 3three-show contract for appearances by The Beatles
Gisela of Swabia, Queen of Germany (1024-39) and Holy Roman Empress as wife of Emperor Conrad II (1027-39), born in Swabia (d. 1041)
